description: CT Chest with IV contrast (with iodinated-contrast pre-medication protocol completed)
radiologist: Dr. Anil Vachani / Dr. Marcus Lim (radiology resident)
indication:
Follow-up imaging after ARDS resolution to assess for residual pulmonary parenchymal abnormality. Also: further characterization of the 2.5 cm right upper lobe nodule first identified on 2/16/2027 CXR and seen on subsequent serial CXRs through 2/18/2027.

Patient is post extubation (2/27/2027) and now medically stable for transport. Pre-medication for iodinated contrast allergy was completed per Hopkins Radiology protocol: prednisone 50 mg PO at 13, 7, and 1 hour prior to procedure; diphenhydramine 50 mg PO 1 hour prior. No allergic reaction during contrast administration.
technique: Multidetector CT of the chest with IV iodinated contrast (pulmonary arterial phase + delayed venous phase). Thin section (1 mm) reconstructions through the lung parenchyma; 5 mm axial reconstructions through the chest; coronal and sagittal reformats; targeted thin sections through the right upper lobe nodule.
findings:
LUNG PARENCHYMA:
- Resolution of prior bilateral interstitial markings — minimal residual fibrotic-appearing reticulation in the dependent lower lobes posteriorly; no current findings of active ARDS.
- No new or recurrent consolidation. No new ground-glass opacity.
- No focal mass or new nodule beyond the previously documented right upper lobe lesion (see below).
- No miliary nodules. No tree-in-bud pattern. No cavitation.

RIGHT UPPER LOBE NODULE (REFERENCE LESION):
- **A solitary 2.5 cm well-circumscribed solid pulmonary nodule is again demonstrated in the apical segment of the right upper lobe.** Compared with the 2/16/2027 CXR, the nodule has not changed in size and is stable in appearance over the 4-week interval.
- Margins: relatively smooth, with a few subtle spiculations along the lateral border (best appreciated on the thin-section axial cuts).
- Internal characteristics: solid throughout; no central cavitation; no calcification; no fat content; no air bronchograms within the nodule.
- Contrast enhancement: nodule enhances homogeneously with IV contrast (enhancement ~40 HU, which is in the indeterminate-to-suspicious range for pulmonary nodules — pulmonary malignancies typically enhance >15 HU above baseline, which is met here).
- Surrounding parenchyma: no adjacent consolidation, no associated lymphangitic spread, no satellite nodules.
- Hilar and mediastinal lymph nodes: A few subcentimeter mediastinal nodes are noted (largest 8 mm in subcarinal station), nonspecific in size but not pathologically enlarged.

PLEURA: Small residual bilateral pleural effusions, decreased from prior. No pneumothorax.

MEDIASTINUM: Cardiac silhouette within normal limits. Aortic atherosclerotic calcification, age-appropriate. Trace pericardial effusion.

CHEST WALL / BONES / SOFT TISSUES: No suspicious osseous lesion. Mild degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.
impression:
1. **STABLE 2.5 CM SOLID RIGHT UPPER LOBE PULMONARY NODULE** with smooth-to-mildly-spiculated borders and homogeneous post-contrast enhancement (~40 HU). Stable in size compared with imaging from 2/16/2027.

2. Resolution of prior ARDS pattern. Small residual bilateral pleural effusions, decreased.
3. No new pulmonary process. No mediastinal lymphadenopathy.
4. Trace pericardial effusion, likely chronic/post-inflammatory.
comparison: Prior portable CXRs from 2/16/2027, 2/17/2027, and 2/18/2027 documented the same right upper lobe nodule. No earlier dedicated chest CT in this institution. The 2010 outpatient CXR (Hopkins Bayview Pulm/ID clinic) was reviewed and is clear of any nodule, suggesting the lesion developed in the interval between 2010 and 2027 (a 17-year gap).
